#+TITLE: Batch Figure Generation Tutorial
#+OPTIONS: toc:nil todo:nil num:nil author:nil

* What You Will Build

Three publication-quality PDF figures from a single command:

1. A convergence plot (oracle calls vs force)
2. A 2D PES contour with NEB path overlay
3. An RFF quality comparison (two-panel)

All from one TOML config file and pre-existing HDF5 data.

* Prerequisites

#+begin_src bash
pip install rgpycrumbs chemparseplot h5py plotnine
#+end_src

You need HDF5 files produced by ~chemgp-core~ examples. The default pipeline
stores them in an ~output/~ directory.

* Write the TOML Config

Create a file ~my_figures.toml~:

#+begin_src toml
[defaults]
input_dir = "output"
output_dir = "output"

[[plots]]
input = "leps_minimize.h5"
output = "leps_convergence.pdf"
type = "convergence"

[[plots]]
input = "leps_neb.h5"
output = "leps_surface.pdf"
type = "surface"

[[plots]]
input = "leps_rff.h5"
output = "leps_rff_quality.pdf"
type = "rff"
#+end_src

Each ~[[plots]]~ entry maps one HDF5 input to one PDF output. The ~type~ field
selects the plt-gp subcommand (convergence, surface, rff, etc.).

Paths in ~input~ and ~output~ resolve relative to ~input_dir~ and ~output_dir~
respectively. Both default to the directory containing the TOML file.

* Generate the Figures

#+begin_src bash
rgpycrumbs chemgp plt-gp batch --config my_figures.toml
#+end_src

Output:

#+begin_example
INFO - [1/3] convergence: output/leps_minimize.h5 -> output/leps_convergence.pdf
INFO - [2/3] surface: output/leps_neb.h5 -> output/leps_surface.pdf
INFO - [3/3] rff: output/leps_rff.h5 -> output/leps_rff_quality.pdf
INFO - Done: 3 ok, 0 skipped, 0 failed
#+end_example

* Customizing Entries

Extra keys in a ~[[plots]]~ entry pass through as CLI flags. For example, to
override figure dimensions and energy clamping:

#+begin_src toml
[[plots]]
input = "mb_neb.h5"
output = "mb_surface.pdf"
type = "surface"
width = 10.0
height = 8.0
clamp_lo = -200
clamp_hi = 50
#+end_src

Any key that matches a CLI option on the corresponding subcommand (with
underscores replacing hyphens) works here.

* Landscape Entries

The ~landscape~ type uses ~source_dir~ instead of ~input~, because it reads .con
and .dat files rather than HDF5:

#+begin_src toml
[[plots]]
source_dir = "output"
output = "leps_landscape.pdf"
type = "landscape"
#+end_src

This delegates to ~rgpycrumbs chemgp plt-neb~ under the hood.
